<!-- GENERATED by `mix codemojex.stories` from echo/apps/codemojex/test/stories/economy_story_test.exs — do not edit by hand. -->

# Economy

> **Feature delivered — acceptance criteria.** Generated from the as-built BDD tests in
> `test/stories/economy_story_test.exs`. Every scenario below is an ExUnit test driving the
> real Codemojex game, so the catalogue cannot drift from the code.

**10 scenarios.**

## Scenario: diamonds convert to keys at a fixed 10:1, floored

- **Given** the fixed conversion rate of 10 diamonds per key
- **Then** 283 diamonds yield 28 keys (the remainder does not round up)

## Scenario: a diamond amount renders as USD at 1.2 cents each

- **Given** the price of a diamond is 1.2 US cents
- **Then** 283 diamonds is $3.40 and 100 diamonds is $1.20

## Scenario: the whole pool goes to the single highest score

- **Given** a finished board with one clear leader
- **When** the pool settles winner-take-all
- **Then** the leader takes the entire diamond pool

## Scenario: a tie for first splits the pool evenly

- **Given** two players tied at the top score
- **When** the pool settles winner-take-all
- **Then** the two leaders split the pool and the rest get nothing

## Scenario: settlement is pure: re-running pays identically

- **Given** a board and a pool
- **Then** two settlements of the same inputs are equal

## Scenario: the sealed top-K split pays each rank its configured weight share

- **Given** a full board and the default [40,25,15,12,8] split over a 1000 pool
- **When** the pool settles top-K (K = 5)
- **Then** only the top 5 are paid, each its weight share of the pool

## Scenario: a configured split can differ from the default

- **Given** a two-rank room split [70,30] over a 1000 pool
- **When** the pool settles by the room's own split
- **Then** the two leaders take 70% and 30%

## Scenario: the split normalizes when fewer players than weights are present

- **Given** only two players but a five-weight split
- **When** the pool settles top-K with the default [40,25,15,12,8]
- **Then** only the assigned ranks are paid and the share normalizes over 40+25

## Scenario: the sealed split is pure: re-running pays identically

- **Given** a board, a pool, and a split
- **Then** two settlements of the same inputs are equal

## Scenario: the lobby progress bar is the best score as a percent of 600

- **Then** 600 is 100%, 180 is 30%, 80 is 13.33%
